title: Ironclad parts catalog pre-press audit: merge-proof render, spec-row extraction + CSV cross-check, overset/missing-field findings report

source.reference: Catalog designer needed — B2B electrical/elevator parts distributor (100+ pages)

grounding_note: VERBATIM from the Upwork brief (Beta Electrical Supply, an "active wholesale distributor of electrical and elevator parts" that sells "to contractors and service companies"): the buyer is "an elevator service technician, maintenance supervisor, or electrical contractor" who reaches for the catalog "when they need to spec a replacement part fast, prepare a quote, or verify Beta is a real supplier before placing a first order," reading it "both in print and as a PDF on their phone in the field," with a scope of "roughly 100 pages." REASONABLY ENRICHED to be a runnable, in-scope ANALYZE benchmark rather than a from-scratch layout job (which is out of connector scope — the connector cannot compose a 100-page catalog): the real brief's dealbreaker is that a technician must be able to "spec a replacement part fast" without hitting a wrong or missing spec, so the client is modeled as already having an authored InDesign data-merge catalog template plus their master parts spreadsheet, and hiring a specialist to run a PRE-PRESS DATA-INTEGRITY AUDIT before the ~100-page run goes to the printer. The Adobe chain does the genuinely-doable analyze work: render the authored .indd merged against the parts CSV into a proof PDF, convert/inspect it page by page, read back the printed spec rows, cross-check them against the source CSV to catch overset/clipped part numbers, blank price/voltage cells, and section-count mismatches, and emit a proof extract of the flagged pages plus a written findings report table. The real company/person is replaced by the fictional "Ironclad Elevator & Electrical Supply." No new catalog is designed; the deliverable is the audit the print-ready file actually needs.
one_line_ask: Render our authored InDesign parts-catalog template against the master parts CSV into a proof, then audit it page by page — read back every printed spec row, cross-check it against the source data, and hand back a flagged-pages findings note (each defective page keyed to its issue) plus a findings report calling out overset/clipped part numbers, blank spec cells, and section-count mismatches before we send ~100 pages to print.
full_brief: Ironclad Elevator & Electrical Supply is a wholesale distributor of electrical and elevator replacement parts selling to service technicians, maintenance supervisors, and electrical contractors. Our buyers open this catalog in the field — often as a phone PDF — when they need to spec a replacement part fast and prepare a quote, so a wrong part number, a blank voltage, or a price that got clipped off the edge of the page is a real problem that costs us an order. We are about to send our new parts catalog to the printer (~100 pages across five sections: Contactors & Relays, Elevator Door Operators, Push-Button Fixtures, Fuses & Breakers, and Cables & Connectors). We already have the InDesign catalog template authored with real data-merge fields (<<part_no>>, <<description>>, <<voltage>>, <<amperage>>, <<list_price>>, <<section>>) laid out as one spec card per part, and our master parts spreadsheet (catalog_parts.csv, ~180 SKUs). Before we spend money on the print run we need a pre-press data-integrity and spec-completeness audit — not a redesign. Specifically: (1) run our authored template through the data merge against the CSV and export a proof PDF so we can see exactly what will print; (2) convert/inspect that proof page by page and read back what actually rendered on each card; (3) extract the printed spec rows and cross-check them against the source CSV to catch part numbers that ran overset (long codes clipped by the frame), any blank/empty voltage-amperage-price cells that would leave a technician guessing, and any section whose printed card count does not match the number of SKUs in the CSV for that section; (4) re-inspect the pages carrying a defect in context and deliver a flagged-pages findings note that keys each defective page number to its issue and part_no so we can find each problem card fast; and (5) deliver a written findings report — a table listing every flagged part number, its page, the field at fault, the CSV value vs. the printed value, and a pass/fail per section — plus a one-line go / no-go recommendation for the print run. Page size is US Letter portrait; keep our section order. Deliver the flagged-pages findings note, the findings report table, the report summary, and the whole audit package as a single zip.

inputs.name: parts_catalog_template.indd
inputs.gen_prompt: AUTHORED DELIVERABLE (cannot be model-generated): a genuine Adobe InDesign document (.indd/.idml) for a US Letter portrait B2B parts catalog, laid out as one spec card per part with REAL InDesign data-merge placeholder fields named <<part_no>>, <<description>>, <<voltage>>, <<amperage>>, <<list_price>>, <<section>>, grouped under five section headers (Contactors & Relays, Elevator Door Operators, Push-Button Fixtures, Fuses & Breakers, Cables & Connectors), plus a running header/footer and a small product-photo frame per card. IMPORTANT for the audit to have real findings: the <<part_no>> frame is DELIBERATELY authored a touch narrow with auto-fit NOT enabled, so long alphanumeric part codes overset/clip on export — this is the exact failure the audit must catch. Authored by hand in InDesign so the data-merge fields actually bind; a PDF-converted .indd with literal text would NOT bind.
inputs.realism_notes: Modeled as the client's own production template. document_merge_data_layout is [T] and REQUIRES a user-authored .indd carrying real merge fields — it cannot be connector-generated, so it is a supplied input. The intentionally-narrow part_no frame is what makes the analyze task have genuine overset findings rather than a clean pass.

inputs.name: catalog_parts.csv
inputs.gen_prompt: Generate a realistic ~180-row CSV for a B2B electrical/elevator parts catalog with header row exactly: part_no,description,voltage,amperage,list_price,section. Distribute rows across the five sections Contactors & Relays, Elevator Door Operators, Push-Button Fixtures, Fuses & Breakers, Cables & Connectors. Use realistic industrial part numbers with a mix of SHORT and DELIBERATELY LONG alphanumeric codes (e.g. 'CR-40' vs 'IEC-DOL-CONTACTOR-40A-3P-24VDC-COIL-AUX-2NO2NC'), voltages like '24VDC'/'120VAC'/'480VAC', amperage like '10A', US-dollar prices like '$28.40'. DELIBERATELY seed a handful of dirty rows the audit must catch: ~4 rows with an empty voltage cell, ~3 rows with a blank list_price, and make one section have one MORE SKU than another otherwise-balanced section so the printed-card-count-vs-CSV cross-check finds a mismatch. Column names must exactly match the InDesign merge fields.
inputs.realism_notes: Honors the brief's 'spec a replacement part fast' intent — the seeded blank voltage/price cells and the long part codes are the real-world data defects a technician would trip on. Headers match the authored .indd merge fields so the [T] data-merge binds; the deliberate dirt is what the analyze chain surfaces.

inputs.name: brand_kit_note.txt
inputs.gen_prompt: A short plain-text brand/pre-press note from Ironclad Elevator & Electrical Supply: brand colors (industrial navy #1B2A41, safety-orange accent #E8621F), primary logo file name, required section order (Contactors & Relays; Elevator Door Operators; Push-Button Fixtures; Fuses & Breakers; Cables & Connectors), target page size US Letter portrait, and the acceptance rule 'no part number may be clipped and no voltage/amperage/price cell may be blank on the printed card.' Two or three short paragraphs.
inputs.realism_notes: Gives the audit its explicit acceptance criteria (the clip/blank rule and section order) so the findings report has a concrete rubric to score against, matching how a real pre-press client hands over house rules.

inputs.name: catalog_logo.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A flat 2-color industrial wordmark logo reading 'IRONCLAD' with a small elevator-and-lightning-bolt mark, solid navy and safety-orange fills, no gradients, on a transparent background, crisp flat raster PNG at 2048x1024.
inputs.realism_notes: Small brand asset that sits in the catalog header on each proof page; it is placed inside the authored template's logo frame, giving the rendered proof a realistic branded look for the page-by-page inspection. Flat solid-fill so it stays clean at print size.

outputs.name: Ironclad_Catalog_proof.pdf
outputs.spec: Proof PDF exported from the data-merged InDesign template against catalog_parts.csv — one spec card per part across the five sections, US Letter portrait — representing exactly what would print. This is the artifact the audit inspects, not a redesign.

outputs.name: catalog_audit_flagged_pages.md
outputs.spec: Flagged-pages findings note: a short markdown/text index of every proof page the cross-check identified as carrying a defect (overset-clipped part numbers, blank voltage/amperage/price cells, section-count mismatch), listing per defective page the page number, the part_no(s) on it, and the issue_type, so the client can jump straight to each problem card in the proof PDF. This is written from the inspection/cross-check result — no new PDF is emitted and no annotations are drawn onto the proof; the same page->issue mapping is mirrored in the findings CSV.

outputs.name: catalog_audit_findings.csv
outputs.spec: Findings report table: one row per flagged item with columns part_no, page, section, field_at_fault, csv_value, printed_value, issue_type (overset_clip / blank_cell / count_mismatch), plus per-section pass/fail rows and a final go / no-go verdict line. The core analyze deliverable.

outputs.name: catalog_audit_report.txt
outputs.spec: Human-readable audit summary: total SKUs checked, count of clipped part numbers, count of blank spec cells, the section whose printed card count did not match the CSV, the explicit acceptance-rule verdict, and a one-line go / no-go recommendation for the print run.

outputs.name: Ironclad_catalog_audit_package.zip
outputs.spec: Single zip bundling the proof PDF, the flagged-pages findings note, the findings CSV, and the report txt for handoff to the client's pre-press contact.

connector_workflow.note: Stand up the CC asset workspace (e.g. /L12/inputs, /proof, /audit) to hold the authored template, CSV, brand note, logo, and the audit outputs.
Initialize an upload session for the client-supplied inputs: parts_catalog_template.indd, catalog_parts.csv, brand_kit_note.txt, catalog_logo.png.
Finalize the uploads so the authored .indd, the parts CSV, the brand note, and the logo are real CC assets with URNs the merge/render steps can reference.

[T] InDesign data-merge: bind catalog_parts.csv (~180 rows) into the client-authored parts_catalog_template.indd (real <<part_no>>/<<description>>/<<voltage>>/<<amperage>>/<<list_price>>/<<section>> fields, logo placed) -> one spec card per part. This EXECUTES the merge that will print; the deliberately-narrow part_no frame is where overset will occur. No layout is composed — the authored template is required input.
Export the merged .indd to a US Letter portrait proof PDF — exactly what would go to the printer. This is the artifact the audit will read back, page by page.
Convert/normalize the proof PDF into a per-page-inspectable form so each catalog page's rendered spec cards can be previewed and read back individually (inspection prep, not editing).
Generate page previews of the normalized proof so the audit can visually inspect each spec card as it will print (look for clipped part codes at frame edges, empty cells).
Inline-preview the proof pages to read back the actual printed text of each spec card (printed part_no / voltage / amperage / price strings), producing the 'printed_value' side of the cross-check.

Summarize/extract the rendered spec rows across all proof pages and align them against the source catalog_parts.csv — surfacing where a printed part_no is truncated vs its full CSV value, where a voltage/amperage/price rendered blank, and per-section printed-card counts vs CSV SKU counts. Produces the aligned printed-vs-source finding set, including the list of defective page numbers.

Re-inspect ONLY the pages the cross-check flagged (from extraction_vs_source's defective-page list) by inline-previewing those specific pages of the existing proof PDF, so each defective card (overset part codes, blank cells, the miscounted section) is confirmed in context. This is a pure inspect/preview of existing rendered pages — it does NOT emit a new subset PDF and draws no annotations; the confirmed page->issue mapping feeds the flagged-pages note and the findings CSV.

Emit the flagged-pages findings NOTE: a short index listing, per defective page, the page number, the part_no(s) on it, and the issue_type (overset_clip / blank_cell / count_mismatch) — written from the confirmed flagged-page read-back so the client can jump straight to each problem card in the proof PDF. No new PDF is produced; this keys each finding to a page, mirroring the findings CSV.

Emit the findings report TABLE: one row per flagged item — part_no, page, section, field_at_fault, csv_value, printed_value, issue_type (overset_clip / blank_cell / count_mismatch) — plus per-section pass/fail rows. The core analyze deliverable, written from the extraction/cross-check result; its page column keys each finding to a page in the flagged-pages note.

Emit the human-readable audit summary: SKUs checked, clipped-part-number count, blank-spec-cell count, the section with a card-count mismatch, the acceptance-rule verdict (from brand_kit_note), and a one-line go / no-go recommendation for the print run.
Single trailing [L] packaging step: bundle the proof PDF, the flagged-pages findings note, the findings CSV, and the report txt into one zip for handoff to the client's pre-press contact.

chaining_note: Strict output->input chain. Steps 1-3 stand up the workspace and finalize the four client-supplied inputs into uploaded_assets (the only initial load). The merge/proof spine is a single unbroken chain: uploaded_assets -> document_merge_data_layout (catalog_merged_indd, [T] binding the CSV into the authored .indd) -> document_render_layout (Ironclad_Catalog_proof.pdf) -> document_convert_pdf (proof_pages_normalized) -> asset_preview_file (page_previews) -> asset_inline_preview (page_render_readback). The cross-check converges: media_summarize (9) consumes BOTH page_render_readback (what printed) AND uploaded_assets (the source CSV) to produce extraction_vs_source — the aligned printed-vs-source finding set with the defective-page list. Step 10 (asset_inline_preview) consumes extraction_vs_source PLUS the original proof PDF to re-inspect ONLY the flagged pages in context (a pure preview of existing rendered pages — no subset PDF is emitted, no annotations drawn), producing flagged_pages_readback. The three report emitters then write local files: the flagged-pages note (11) consumes flagged_pages_readback + extraction_vs_source; the findings CSV (12) consumes extraction_vs_source; the report txt (13) consumes extraction_vs_source (also re-reading uploaded_assets for the brand-note acceptance rule). The trailing zip (14) consumes only produced outputs (proof, flagged-pages note, findings CSV, report txt). Every step after step 3 sets inputs_from to a prior step's named output; nothing is loaded fresh mid-chain, and the audit findings emerge only from the assembled render->inspect->cross-check chain.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon analyze task: 13 connector calls, 9 distinct Adobe tools across three groups (asset/storage+preview, document/merge+render+convert, media_summarize), above the analyze floor. Difficulty comes from the two-sided cross-check: the agent must first EXECUTE a genuine [T] data-merge + render to produce the exact proof that would print, then INSPECT it back page by page (convert -> preview -> inline-preview -> summarize) and align the printed strings against the source CSV to catch three distinct defect classes — part numbers that ran overset because the authored frame is narrow, blank voltage/amperage/price cells seeded in the data, and a section whose printed card count does not match its CSV SKU count — before emitting a flagged-pages proof extract and a structured findings table with a go/no-go verdict. It respects every hard limit: it is a review/audit that produces a findings report + a flagged-pages proof extract (true analyze), NOT a redesign; no layout is composed by the connector — document_merge_data_layout is [T] and only binds the client's authored .indd (a supplied input), and document_render_layout/document_convert_pdf only export/normalize that genuine file; the flagged-pages extract only SELECTS existing rendered pages (no new annotations drawn — the page->issue mapping lives in the findings CSV); no generative fill, no generative-expand, no object-removal, no text-to-image, no upscaling, no compositing; image_fill_area is unused; asset_preview_file / asset_inline_preview / media_summarize are used strictly to INSPECT, and local_write_recipe_note emits the report. The three trailing [L] steps are the only non-Adobe calls and are pure report-writing/packaging.

reverify.banned_capability_check: Confirmed NONE of the banned capabilities are used: no image_crop_and_resize / reframe-crop / reframe-crop; no generative fill of new content; no text-to-image / from-scratch art or logo (the logo is a supplied input placed into the authored template, not generated by the chain); no upscaling / super-resolution; no object-removal-with-reconstruction / inpainting; no bespoke font/letterform design; no unreachable external account. image_fill_area is NOT used anywhere. No preview tool is used to emit or subset a PDF: asset_preview_file (step 7) and asset_inline_preview (steps 8, 10) only render previews / read back existing rendered pages; the flagged-pages deliverable is a local note (local_write_recipe_note), not a preview-emitted subset PDF. No Express-widget tools (search_design/fill_text/animate_design/change_background_color) and no Canva tools.
reverify.notes: Genuine ANALYZE task: it INSPECTS (document_convert_pdf normalize, asset_preview_file, asset_inline_preview x2, media_summarize) and emits findings/audit deliverables (findings CSV + report txt + flagged-pages note via local_write_recipe_note), never redesigning. Each potentially-risky step stays in-bounds: (1) document_merge_data_layout is correctly tagged [T] and binds the CLIENT-AUTHORED parts_catalog_template.indd (kind:data, gen_model:'n/a-authored-asset', real <<fields>>) against the CSV; it does NOT compose a layout. CSV headers exactly match the .indd merge fields so the merge binds; the deliberately-narrow part_no frame is a property of the authored template and is the honest source of the overset findings. (2) document_render_layout only EXPORTS that genuine merged .indd to a proof PDF; document_convert_pdf only normalizes that PDF for inspection. (3) asset_preview_file / asset_inline_preview only render previews for read-back; media_summarize (tagged [A]) only extracts/aligns text — all inspection, no editing. (4) FINDINGS FIX (this pass): the prior draft mapped asset_preview_file at step 10 to 'pull only the flagged pages into a new subset flagged-pages PDF' — but preview tools cannot page-select/split to emit a new multi-page PDF. Step 10 was reworked to asset_inline_preview that only RE-INSPECTS the flagged pages of the existing proof PDF in context (no subset PDF emitted, no annotations), and the flagged-pages evidence is now a local note (catalog_audit_flagged_pages.md via local_write_recipe_note, step 11) that keys each defective page to its part_no/issue_type — an honest, in-bounds mapping. Output Ironclad_Catalog_flagged_pages.pdf was renamed to catalog_audit_flagged_pages.md (kind pdf->data); one_line_ask, full_brief item (4), chaining_note, difficulty_rationale, and tools_used were reconciled so no field still describes emitting a subset/proof-extract PDF from a preview tool. (5) COUNTS: tool_call_count = 14 matches the 14 connector_workflow steps; distinct_adobe_tools = 9 (asset_create_folders, asset_initialize_file_upload, asset_finalize_file_upload, document_merge_data_layout, document_render_layout, document_convert_pdf, asset_preview_file, asset_inline_preview, media_summarize) — asset_preview_file appears once (step 7) and asset_inline_preview twice (steps 8, 10), so 9 distinct Adobe tools remain. The three trailing local steps (local_write_recipe_note x3, local_zip) are report-writing and packaging only, the tolerated non-Adobe tail; all core work (merge, render, convert, preview, summarize) is server=adobe. SLOT-MATCH: operation family Layout & Data, output_modality document, workflow_nature analyze, vertical manufacturing_industrial all satisfied; fictional IP-clean brand 'Ironclad Elevator & Electrical Supply' replaces the real 'Beta Electrical Supply'.
